--- SOURCE 1: How can you manage stakeholders in UAV projects? ---
URL: https://www.linkedin.com/advice/3/how-can-you-manage-stakeholders-uav-projects-skills-drones

SUMMARY:
1. Identify stakeholders ; 2. Analyze stakeholders ; 3. Engage stakeholders ; 4. Communicate with stakeholders ; 5. Manage stakeholder expectations.



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- SOURCE 2: FAA Announces Stakeholder Members of UAS Detection ... ---
URL: https://www.faa.gov/newsroom/faa-announces-stakeholder-members-uas-detection-and-mitigation-aviation-rulemaking

SUMMARY:
Fifty-eight aviation stakeholders will soon develop recommendations for the expanded detection and mitigation of unmanned aircraft systems.



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- SOURCE 3: Multistakeholder Process: Unmanned Aircraft Systems ---
URL: https://www.ntia.gov/other-publication/2016/multistakeholder-process-unmanned-aircraft-systems

SUMMARY:
This web page provides details on the NTIA-convened multistakeholder process concerning privacy, transparency, and accountability issues



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- SOURCE 4: Unmanned Aircraft Systems Standardization Collaborative ... ---
URL: https://www.ansi.org/standards-coordination/collaboratives-activities/unmanned-aircraft-systems-collaborative

SUMMARY:
*   www.ansi.org 4**coveo_visitorId**Saves information of actions that have been carried out by the user during the current visit to the website, including searches with keywords included.**Maximum Storage Duration**: 1 year**Type**: HTTP Cookie  **__coveo.analytics.history**Necessary in order to optimize the website's search-bar function. Check out ANSI's FAQs for U.S. TAG members, U.S. TAG leaders, and others with an interest in ISO international standards development activities. The ANSI UASSC’s mission is to coordinate and accelerate the development of the standards and conformity assessment programs needed to facilitate the safe integration of unmanned aircraft systems (UAS) – commonly known as drones – into the national airspace system (NAS) of the United States.

FULL CONTENT:
ANSI Unmanned Aircraft Systems Standardization Collaborative – UASSC

===============

--- SOURCE 5: Stakeholder engagement on Urban Air Mobility and assessing ... ---
URL: https://hub.easa.europa.eu/crt/docs/viewcrdattachment/cid_171322/aid_3498/fmd_cd5dca23ec7e22e4462ca7d5e121de28

SUMMARY:
Topics covered. 1. Key stakeholders in Urban Air Mobility (UAM). 2. Perceptions from stakeholders on acceptable use cases and the advancement of UAM.

FULL CONTENT:
This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 Stakeholder engagement on Urban Air Mobility and assessing public perceptions This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 Topics covered 1.
Key stakeholders in Urban Air Mobility (UAM) 2.
Perceptions from stakeholders on acceptable use cases and the advancement of UAM operations 3.
Current opinion from European citizens on UAM and use cases 4.
Methods for engagement (Audience participation) 5.
Increased public acceptance for this new technology (Audience participation) Learning objectives • List the stakeholders who should be consulted on drone operations • Advise ways in which the public can be engaged on the topic of UAM and how to increase their acceptance of this new form of transport Topics & learnings from this session This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 Name stakeholders who should be engaged if you are planning a medical drone operation This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 Stakeholders in UAM • Aviation regulators • EASA • Civil Aviation Authority • Aviation Navigation Authority • Police / Military • Controllers • Drone operators • Air Traffic Control • Infrastructure • Take-off / landing infrastructure • Communications operators • Energy suppliers • Industry • Public • Commercial • Consultants • City planners • Transport operators • Architects • Society • Public • Community groups • Environmental groups • Authorities • City • Regional Industry reps Aviation regulators Drone operators City reps Environmental groups Media Comms Energy suppliers Transport operators Physical infrastructure providers Air Traffic Control UAM Community This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 Stakeholder engagement Prior knowledge levels … This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 Use cases … This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 Expert perspective on advancing UAM This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 • Lack of specific test areas.
• Technology readiness, minimising risks, communication infrastructure, public acceptance.
• Regulations and standards are missing, in particular for passenger eVTOLs and fully autonomous aircraft.
• ‘Holistic technologic aspect’ remains a key challenge, which includes how to build UAM into existing ground infrastructure (adapt cities for drone flights).
• Drones have to be able to land anywhere e.g. to deliver a defibrillator anywhere. This creates challenges from an aviation security perspective, as drone corridors are no longer useful.
What are the barriers to innovation?
This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 • Economies of scale have to be developed in order for drones to be commercially attractive.
• Architects need to take this new form of mobility into account in their designs, e.g. open windows for drone deliveries in high rise buildings.
• Well defined regulations, testing and certification, infrastructure, good network of cellular coverage.
• Vision is for the transportation, digital infrastructure, physical infrastructure, air mobility industry to be created with the user in mind. Also the industry needs to focus on the youth programme outreach in the future workforce.
What are the next steps for integration?
This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 Questionnaire of 1,104 Citizens from 6 countries Female Male 50% 50% Age group Percentage 18-30 16.5% 31-40 16.5% 41-50 17% 51-60 17% 61-70 16.5% 70+ 16.5% Germany – Nordhessen region Luxembourg – whole country Netherlands – whole country Sweden – Stockholm, Goteborg, Norrköping and Linköping Finland – Uusimaa region Norway – Stavanger, Oslo, Bergen This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 Knowledge of UAM ...
Public engagement This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 Exposure to drones This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 Use cases… This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 Use cases… This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 Use cases… This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 Comfort and trust … This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 Location of operation… Near to residence?
Near to work?
This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 Location of operation… Suitability of location for eVTOL landing site This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 Key findings from engagement to date • Citizens are either ‘not knowledgeable at all’ about UAM or are ‘slightly knowledgeable’. A high proportion are ‘undecided’ about drones which is reflective of their lack of knowledge.
• There is a link between news reports and levels of public acceptability. The media plays a large role in this.
• Acceptable use cases are infrastructure maintenance, land or building surveying & environmental monitoring.
• Non-urgent medical use cases are considered less acceptable than urgent ones.
• Social inequality appeared as a high concern for citizens if delivery drones and passenger eVTOLs are introduced in the airspace.
• Technical stakeholders feel that regulations are currently the most limiting factor in the advancement of UAM. There is a need for an easier process to enable test flights for drones and certification of drone operators should be mandatory.
This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 What strategies could be used to engage the public on this topic?
Questions What concerns do you have with Urban Air Mobility?
What factors would increase public acceptance of drones?
What benefits do you think Urban Air Mobility could bring?
This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 Initial learnings from engagement activities • Engage with citizens as early as possible!
• Be inclusive in your engagement.
• Encourage women in the industry.
• Speak to the media.
• Invite stakeholders and the public to your demonstrations.
• Support uses that benefit the whole of society.
• Regulate, certify and monitor!
This project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon. 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 101006601 Thank You!

enriched_points.expanded_content: Incorporate diverse perspectives from regulators, manufacturers, operators, and the public to foster comprehensive input, enhancing the relevance and effectiveness of UAV standards.
Establish protocols for periodic revisions of UAV standards to adapt to technological advancements and emerging challenges, ensuring ongoing safety and efficiency in operations.
Promote collaborative R&D efforts among stakeholders to drive innovation in UAV technology, addressing safety, efficiency, and environmental concerns through shared knowledge and resources.
Develop flexible regulatory structures that can evolve with technological progress, allowing for rapid integration of new UAV applications while ensuring public safety and compliance.
